Introduction to Knowledge Bases

A knowledge base is an organised collection of information that can be queried and maintained over time. Grove compiles raw sources into a structured wiki using large language models.

Why Use a Knowledge Base?

Knowledge bases help teams share understanding, reduce repeated questions, and maintain a single source of truth. They are especially valuable in organisations where knowledge is scattered across documents, emails, and chat messages.

How Grove Works

Grove ingests documents in various formats including PDF, HTML, and plain text. It converts them to markdown, scores their quality, and then compiles them into a wiki structure that can be browsed in Obsidian or any markdown-compatible tool.
